decalogo.title: Environmental decalogue
decalogo.subtitle: Two lists of best practices to reduce our impact: one for the natural environment around us and one for the digital world we use every day.

card.title: Real impact
card.body: Reduce waste, choose thoughtfully, and share good habits: every daily gesture can become a concrete step toward a cleaner future.

home.subtitle: An authentic experience aboard vintage carriages. Travel through National Parks and the most beautiful villages of the Central Apennines.
home.sponsorsAria: Partners and sponsors of Transiberiana d'Abruzzo
home.heroImageAlt: Historic train crossing a viaduct among the green mountains of Abruzzo

hero.subtitle: Designed in the late nineteenth century, the Sulmona-Isernia railway is a masterpiece of civil engineering that connected mountain communities before becoming Italy's most iconic tourist rail line.

timeline.title: Work begins
timeline.desc: Construction starts on the Sulmona-Isernia line. The engineering challenge is enormous: the route must overcome steep gradients by carving tunnels through the solid rock of the Apennines.

timeline.title: The inauguration
timeline.desc: The section as far as Cansano and Campo di Giove is inaugurated. The railway immediately becomes the main transport link for goods and passengers between Abruzzo and Molise.

timeline.title: Destruction and rebirth
timeline.desc: During the Second World War the line suffers severe damage because of the Gustav Line. In the 1950s it is rebuilt and modernized, returning to its role as a vital connector.

timeline.title: The suspension
timeline.desc: Regular passenger service is suspended because of high operating costs. People fear the definitive abandonment of the historic line.

timeline.title: The Transiberiana
timeline.desc: Reborn as a tourist railway thanks to Fondazione FS Italiane, it now offers the magic of travelling on vintage trains through the untouched landscapes of the Apennines.

stations.name: Sulmona
stations.desc: The city of sugared almonds and Ovid. Starting point of the historic itinerary.

stations.name: Campo di Giove
stations.desc: At the foot of the Majella, framed by dense woods and breathtaking landscapes.

stations.name: Palena
stations.desc: An isolated station in Quarto Santa Chiara, a realm of untamed nature.

stations.name: Roccaraso
stations.desc: The highest station on the line, famous for mountain tourism in both winter and summer.

stations.name: Castel di Sangro
stations.desc: Town of water and fly fishing, a key hub of the upper Sangro Valley.

stops.title: Stations along the journey
stops.subtitle: After the history, the route continues through villages and plateaus: every stop is a moment to enjoy slowly.

cta.title: Buy your ticket
cta.body: Leave your email and we will let you know as soon as bookings open for the next departures.

form.label: Email
form.success: Thanks! We will let you know as soon as bookings open.

stops.title: Stops
stops.subtitle: Explore the historic stations along Italy's highest and most scenic railway, a ribbon of steel stitching together national parks and nature reserves.

stations.name: Sulmona
stations.desc: The city of sugared almonds and Ovid. Starting point of the historic itinerary.

activities.title: What to do during the stops
activities.subtitle: Choose the season and discover what to do in the towns where the train stops, with suggestions tailored to the length of each stop.
activities.note: Indicative durations: we will update the timetable details as soon as they are available.

seasonCopy.spring: Blossoms and fresh air: gentle walks and slower rhythms.
seasonCopy.summer: Long days: look for shade, fresh water and wide-open views.
seasonCopy.autumn: Warm colors and seasonal flavors: perfect for an easy pace.
seasonCopy.winter: Mountain atmosphere: short activities and warm indoor breaks.

stops.name: Sulmona
stops.timeHint: Ideal before departure or on the way back
stops.desc: The city of sugared almonds and Ovid. Starting point of the historic itinerary.

plans.label: Short stop
plans.mealTip: If it is lunchtime, choose a trattoria in the town center.

spring: Walk through the historic center, alleys and squares.
Coffee break with traditional pastries.
summer: Shaded route along the main street.
Gelato or a cold drink in the square.
autumn: Warm break in a historic cafe.
winter: Porticoes and churches indoors for a quick visit.
Hot chocolate or a local sweet.

plans.label: Relaxed stop
plans.mealTip: With 2-3 hours you can stop for a relaxed lunch.

spring: Longer walk through historic neighborhoods and artisan shops.
A short stroll through the town gardens.
summer: Light aperitif and an evening walk.
Browse local products at a relaxed pace.
autumn: Taste local products and browse the shops.
winter: Visit indoor cultural spaces.
Longer break with a hot drink.

stops.name: Campo di Giove
stops.desc: At the foot of the Majella, framed by dense woods and breathtaking landscapes.

plans.label: Short stop
plans.mealTip: If it is lunchtime, choose a quick option near the station.

spring: Lookout point and photos of the surrounding peaks.
Short walk in the nearby woods.
summer: Panoramic viewpoint with cool air.
Short shaded stretch and a water refill.
autumn: Break in the town center for seasonal flavors.
winter: Snowy views and quick photos.
Warm up with a hot drink.

plans.label: Long stop
plans.mealTip: With 3 hours you can take a walk and then enjoy lunch calmly.

spring: Easy excursion through woods and clearings.
Lunch in a trattoria or a picnic if the weather allows.
summer: Fresh walk in the greenery and some relaxation.
Outdoor lunch or a simple local eatery.
autumn: Walk among the colors of the forest.
winter: Short safe walk with snowy scenery.
Hearty lunch indoors.

stops.name: Palena
stops.desc: An isolated station in Quarto Santa Chiara, a realm of untamed nature.

plans.label: Quick stop
plans.mealTip: If it is lunchtime, go for a quick snack.

spring: Panoramic lookout and photos.
Short walk around the station.
summer: Break in the shade with an open view.
Refill your water and head off again.
autumn: Quick stop in the fresh mountain air.
winter: Snowy panorama from a safe viewpoint.
Hot drink before departure.

plans.label: Short stop
plans.mealTip: With a bit more time, treat yourself to a seated break.

spring: Gentle walk nearby with mountain views.
Coffee break or a snack.
summer: Longer walk with cool air.
Refreshing stop before setting off again.
winter: Short visit to the town center.
Warm break in a bar or mountain lodge.

stops.name: Roccaraso
stops.desc: The highest station on the line, famous for mountain tourism in both winter and summer.

plans.label: Short stop
plans.mealTip: If it is lunchtime, choose a place near the center.

spring: Walk through the town center and mountain views.
Coffee break with a view.
summer: Walk through the village with fresh air.
Refreshing stop and panoramic photos.
winter: Mountain atmosphere and snowy views.
Hot drink or a local dessert.

plans.label: Long stop
plans.mealTip: With 2-3 hours you can enjoy lunch and a full walk.

spring: Longer walk through the town.
Lunch in a trattoria at a relaxed pace.
summer: Route through the greenery and time to relax.
Outdoor lunch or a typical local venue.
winter: Light winter activities or scenic viewpoints.
Warm lunch before heading back.

stops.name: Castel di Sangro
stops.desc: Town of water and fly fishing, a key hub of the upper Sangro Valley.

plans.label: Short stop
plans.mealTip: If it is lunchtime, head for a trattoria near the center.

spring: Walk through the center and photos along the main street.
Coffee break in the square.
summer: Evening walk and gelato.
Cool stop in the shade.
autumn: Walk through lanes and scenic corners.
winter: Historic center and indoor venues.
Hot drink and a relaxing break.

plans.label: Long stop
plans.mealTip: With a long stop you can enjoy lunch without rushing.

spring: Complete tour between the center and green areas.
Relaxed lunch and a final stroll.
summer: Long break with lunch and a walk.
Aperitif if you arrive later in the afternoon.
autumn: Slow route among colors and local shops.
winter: Warm lunch and an easygoing visit.
Cafe stop before returning.

info.subtitle: Everything you need to know before boarding this retro adventure through the mountains of Abruzzo.
info.tripadvisorAria: Open TripAdvisor reviews (opens in a new tab)

rules.title: On-board rules
rules.body: Historic trains require care. Do not lean out of the windows while the train is moving, do not throw rubbish from the train, and behave respectfully toward the vintage materials and other passengers.

faq.q: Is the train heated in winter?
faq.a: Yes, all the vintage carriages are equipped with original restored and working steam heating and radiators.

faq.q: Can I bring my dog on board?
faq.a: Small dogs are allowed free of charge inside the appropriate carrier. Larger dogs require a dedicated ticket.
Yes, although they are the original 1930s ones. For greater comfort we suggest using the toilets available at the stations during longer stops.

faq.q: Can I bring food on board?
faq.a: Absolutely. There is no restaurant car, so bringing your own packed lunch is welcome, especially during longer stops in the villages.

meteo.title: Live weather
meteo.subtitle: Real-time weather conditions along the Transiberiana d'Abruzzo.

empty.title: No data available
empty.dark: The station sensors are sleeping deeply. The weather owl is keeping watch. Try again in a few minutes.
empty.light: The station sensors are sleeping. Try again in a few minutes, the weather bunny is on the job.

error.title: Connection lost
error.dark: The candle has gone out, we cannot reach the sensors. Check the connection and try again.
error.light: We cannot reach the IoT sensors. Check the connection and try again.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.body: The page you are looking for does not exist or has been moved. You can go back to the home page and continue the journey.
